rows.Description & Purpose:
Credits from the SAME legal entity — inter-account transfers between the company's own bank accounts.

FULL COMPANY NAME MATCH (not short root). Extract counterparty name → normalise (remove SDN, BHD, &, CO, (M), PTY, LTD, punctuation, extra spaces, uppercase) → exact match against full company_name.

MUHAFIZ SECURITY == MUHAFIZ SECURITY → own party ✓
MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY ≠ MUHAFIZ SECURITY → NOT own party ✗
DMC TRAVEL == DMC TRAVEL → own party ✓
DMC CONSTRUCTION ≠ DMC TRAVEL → NOT own party ✗

Note: Short root matching (MUHAFIZ, DMC) is used ONLY for related party detection (RP2 → C03/C04), NOT for own party (C01/C02).
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'IBG CREDIT' + [company_name]
CIMB: 'DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT' + [company_name]
CIMB: 'I-FUNDS TR FROM SA' + [account holder]
Maybank: 'TRANSFER TO A/C' + [company_name]

Generic: Any incoming transfer where SENDER name matches account holder's company name after normalisation.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
Exclude if sender is a DIFFERENT entity (even with similar name prefix).
Exclude if classified as related party (C03).
Do NOT rely only on transfer keywords (IBG, DUITNOW, TRANSFER) — counterparty name is the classifier.
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): re.search(r'(IBG CREDIT|DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT|TRANSFER TO A/C|I-FUNDS TR FROM).*' + re.escape(company_root), desc, re.I)
rows.Additional Logic:
Extract full normalised company name for matching.
MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN BHD → normalised = 'MUHAFIZ SECURITY'
DMC TRAVEL AND TOURS SDN BHD → normalised = 'DMC TRAVEL AND TOURS' or 'DMC TRAVEL' (with truncation tolerance)

Match case-insensitive on normalised full name — NOT short root.

If extracted counterparty does not match full company name → proceed to C03, C10, C12, C13. If none match → income (stays in net credits).

Short root (MUHAFIZ, DMC) is for RP2 related party detection only.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If no match → leave as 3rd party income
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Cannot also be C03. C01 takes priority over C03 if company name matches.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Debits transferring money TO the same company's other bank accounts.

Mirror C01 logic for debit side. FULL COMPANY NAME MATCH — same normalisation rules. Short root matching (RP2) is for related party detection (C03/C04) only, not own party.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'TR IBG' + [company_name]
CIMB: 'TR TO C/A' + [company_name]
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C' + [company_name]

Generic: Any outgoing transfer where RECIPIENT name = company's own name after normalisation.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
Exclude if recipient is a DIFFERENT entity (related party).
Note: Some own-party debits are ALSO loan repayments (C11) — dual-tag allowed.
DUAL-TAG RULE: Single transaction can be C02 + C11. Excluded from net debits only ONCE via C02. C11 tracked separately for reporting.
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): re.search(r'(TR IBG|TR TO C/A|TRANSFER FR A/C).*' + re.escape(company_root), desc, re.I)
rows.Additional Logic:
CRITICAL edge cases:
CIMB: 'TR IBG MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN Term loan' = C02 AND C11
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C DMC TRAVEL AND TOUR* 344965' = C02 (ref after * is booking ref, ignore)

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If no match → check related party (C04)
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Can co-exist with C11 (loan repayment). Own-party exclusion from net debits already covers it.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Credits from entities related to the company — directors, shareholders, sister companies, family members.

Purpose keyword is the tiebreaker: same person can be both related party AND customer. Use purpose after * to disambiguate.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
Match incoming credit sender name against related_parties[] list.

CIMB: 'DUITNOW ... MUHAFIZ PRIMA SDN' (sister co)
CIMB: 'IBG CREDIT ... MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY' (sister co)
Maybank: 'TRANSFER TO A/C NUR THAHIRAH NAZIHA*' (director)
Maybank: 'TRANSFER TO A/C MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN* Advance' = C03 (personal funds injected)

Keywords that trigger C03 when sender is related party: 'Advance', 'Advance [company]', 'Return', 'Refund'.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
Exclude own-party (C01 takes priority).
Exclude if purpose keyword indicates customer payment (e.g. 'Umrah payment' from director = customer, not C03).
Surplus refund from factoring (Planworth AUTOPAY CR without F ADVANCE) = NOT C03, stays in net credits.
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): any(re.search(re.escape(rp_name), desc, re.I) for rp_name in related_party_names)
rows.Additional Logic:
Build related_parties list per Tab 4 rules (RP1-RP6).
For travel companies: directors may ALSO be customers — purpose keyword after * disambiguates.
Suggestion: allow optional related_parties list input before running analysis.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If uncertain whether customer or related party → classify as 3rd party income, flag for review
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Must not overlap with C01. C01 checked first.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Debits paid TO related entities — directors, sister companies, family members.

Rule order: C02 → C05 → C04. Salary keywords take priority over related party classification.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
Match outgoing debit recipient against related_parties[] list.

CIMB: 'TR TO C/A MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY' (sister co)
CIMB: 'TR TO C/A SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAM' (director)
CIMB: 'TR IBG DAYANG SITI RAUDZAH' (family)
CIMB: 'I-PYMT TO CCARD SHAHARUDDIN' (director CC)
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C NUR THAHIRAH NAZIHA*' (director, non-salary)
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN*' (non-salary operational = regular expense)
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
Exclude own-party (C02 takes priority).
Exclude salary (C05) — if description contains 'Salary'/'Gaji' keyword.
Exclude statutory payments.
MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN split: Salary = C05, operational expenses (visa, tickets) = regular expense (NOT C04), 'Advance'/'Advance DMC' credits = C03.
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): any(re.search(re.escape(rp_name), desc, re.I) for rp_name in related_party_names) and not is_salary(desc) and not is_own_party(desc)
rows.Additional Logic:
CRITICAL: Purpose keyword after * disambiguates.
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C [person]* Salary' = C05
'TRANSFER FR A/C [same person]* Repayment' = C04
CIMB: 'TR IBG SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMS Instalment' = C04 (director's personal loan)

For behavioural related parties (RP3): analyst reviews final classification.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If related party name found but purpose unclear → classify as related party debit (conservative)
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Must not overlap with C02 or C05. Check C02 first, then C05, then C04.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Salary and wage payments to employees.

CIMB uses BULK AUTOPAY — every 'AUTOPAY DR U20...' is salary.
Maybank uses INDIVIDUAL transfers with salary/gaji keyword.

Staff compensation keywords (all = C05): SALARY, GAJI, STAFF SALARY, STAFF INCENTIVE, STAFF OVERTIME, STAFF BONUS, STAFF ADVANCE, EXTRA SALARY, Guard Salary.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'AUTOPAY DR U20...' (bulk salary batch — always salary)
CIMB: 'TR TO SAVINGS [name] STAFF SALARY'
CIMB: 'TR IBG [name] STAFF INCENTIVE'

Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C [name]* [Month] Salary'
Maybank: 'TRANSFER FR A/C [name]* Gaji'

Generic keywords: SALARY, GAJI, STAFF SALARY, STAFF INCENTIVE, STAFF OVERTIME, STAFF BONUS, STAFF ADVANCE, EXTRA SALARY, Guard Salary
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: 'AUTOPAY CHARGES' (bank processing fees → C24)
EXCLUDE: 'AUTOPAY CR' (incoming credits, not salary)
INCLUDE: 'STAFF INCENTIVE' = C05 (employee compensation, per Q10 decision)
rows.Regex Pattern (Python):
CIMB: r"AUTOPAY DR U\d{4}"
Maybank: r"TRANSFER FR A/C.*\*.*(?:SALARY|GAJI)"
Generic: r"(?:SALARY|GAJI|STAFF SALARY|STAFF INCENTIVE|STAFF OVERTIME|STAFF BONUS|STAFF ADVANCE|EXTRA SALARY|GUARD SALARY)"
rows.Additional Logic:
CIMB AUTOPAY DR = always salary. No keyword needed.
Maybank individual transfers need salary keyword in purpose.

For TR TO SAVINGS (CIMB): NOT automatically salary. Extract recipient name → check related party first (C04) → check salary keywords → if match = C05 → remainder = regular expense. Do not blanket-classify.

Do NOT deduplicate salary with EPF — separate outflows.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If 'TRANSFER FR A/C [name]*' without salary keyword → NOT salary
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Salary takes priority over C04 if salary keyword present. Directors can receive both salary AND other payments — classify each individually.
rows.Impact on Net Cr/Dr: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)

rows.Description & Purpose:
Employees Provident Fund (KWSP / Kumpulan Wang Simpanan Pekerja).

CRITICAL: v3.1 adds MONTH-BY-MONTH coverage check.
For every month where salary_paid > 0, statutory_epf SHOULD be > 0.
Missing months = compliance gap.
Ratio check (11-13%) is SECONDARY — only applies per-month where BOTH exist.
Do NOT compute ratio as total_epf / total_salary across all months — this masks gaps.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
KEYWORD DICTIONARY (search ANY):
• 'KUMPULAN WANG SIMPANAN PEKERJA' (full Malay name)
• 'EPF DPE' (Maybank direct payment entry)
• 'EPF' (abbreviation)
• 'KWSP' (Malay abbreviation)

CIMB: 'I-PAYMENT FPXPAY KUMPULAN WANG SIMPANAN PEKERJA'
Maybank: 'PAYMENT FR A/C EPF DPE *'
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
Exclude own-party transfers mentioning EPF (e.g. 'DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT EPF PAYMENT ... MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN' = inter-account transfer to fund EPF → C01, not C06).

JOMPAY RULE: JomPAY is a payment channel, not a payee. Never classify based on JomPAY biller code alone. Classify ONLY when entity name is visible. If description is just JOMPAY xxxx:xxxxx with no entity name → leave as regular expense.
rows.Additional Logic:
Multiple payments per month = normal (different branches/regions).
Expected: ~11-13% of salary PER MONTH where BOTH exist.

v3.1 COVERAGE CHECK (PRIMARY):
For each month: if salary_paid > 0 but statutory_epf = 0 → GAP.
Report: epf_months_paid / salary_months_active × 100.
List all missing months explicitly.

v3.1 RATIO CHECK (SECONDARY, per-month only):
8-16% = OK. Outside range = WARNING.
Ratio > 16% = possible CATCH_UP (lump-sum for prior missed months).
NEVER compute aggregate ratio across all months.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule:
If not detected but salary exists in ANY month → flag WARNING with list of missing months.
Do NOT silently pass because aggregate ratio looks acceptable.
rows.Cross-Ref Check:
Validate: EPF ÷ salary = 11-13% PER MONTH.
If outside 8-16% → flag anomaly.
v3.1: Coverage check V7 is PRIMARY. Ratio check V3 is SECONDARY.
rows.Impact on Net Cr/Dr: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)

rows.Description & Purpose:
Social Security (PERKESO / Pertubuhan Keselamatan Sosial).

Maybank truncates to 'PERTUBUHAN KESELAMAT' (~20 chars). Match partial.

v3.1: SOCSO is mandatory REGARDLESS of salary quantum.
Previous fallback threshold 'salary > RM50K' was too lenient — removed.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
KEYWORD DICTIONARY:
• 'PERTUBUHAN KESELAMATAN SOSIAL' (full)
• 'PERTUBUHAN KESELAMAT' (truncated)
• 'SOCSO'
• 'PERKESO'

CIMB: 'I-PAYMENT FPXPAY PERTUBUHAN KESELAMATAN SOSIAL'
Maybank: 'PAYMENT FR A/C CP_... * PERTUBUHAN KESELAMAT'
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
JomPAY global rule applies (see C06).
Add exclusion patterns to prevent false matches with similar words.
rows.Additional Logic:
Expected: ~2-3% of salary PER MONTH where BOTH exist.
Small companies may pay quarterly.

v3.1 COVERAGE CHECK (PRIMARY):
For each month: if salary_paid > 0 but statutory_socso = 0 → GAP.
Report: socso_months_paid / salary_months_active × 100.
List all missing months explicitly.

v3.1 RATIO CHECK (SECONDARY, per-month only):
1-5% = OK. Outside range = WARNING.
Ratio > 5% = possible CATCH_UP.
NEVER compute aggregate ratio across all months.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule:
If not detected but salary exists in ANY month → flag WARNING with list of missing months.
v3.1: Previous threshold 'salary > RM50K' removed — SOCSO mandatory regardless of salary quantum.
rows.Cross-Ref Check:
Validate: SOCSO ÷ salary = 2-3% PER MONTH.
If outside 1-5% → flag.
v3.1: Coverage check V8 is PRIMARY. Ratio check V4 is SECONDARY.
rows.Impact on Net Cr/Dr: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)

rows.Description & Purpose:
Income tax (LHDN / Lembaga Hasil Dalam Negeri).

CRITICAL: Do NOT match personal names containing 'HASIL' — e.g. HASILA BINTI HASHIM = customer name, NOT LHDN. Must match full phrase 'LEMBAGA HASIL DALAM NEGERI' or abbreviation 'LHDN'.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
KEYWORD DICTIONARY:
• 'LEMBAGA HASIL DALAM NEGERI' (full phrase)
• 'LHDN' (abbreviation)

CIMB: 'I-PAYMENT FPXPAY LEMBAGA HASIL DALAM NEGERI MAL'
Maybank: 'PAYMENT FR A/C ... LEMBAGA HASIL' or via JomPAY
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: 'SERVICE TAX 8% SST' (bank SST, not income tax → C24)
EXCLUDE: Person names containing 'HASIL' (HASILA, HASIL BIN, etc.)

JomPAY global rule applies (see C06).
rows.Additional Logic:
Includes CP204 monthly installments AND lump-sum balance payments.
May be paid from different account — absence ≠ non-compliance.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule:
If LHDN not detected across entire period but salary exists → flag WARNING.
May legitimately be paid from different account, but absence should be reported, not silently ignored.
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Do NOT match partial 'HASIL' — must be full phrase or abbreviation.
rows.Impact on Net Cr/Dr: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)

rows.Description & Purpose: Human Resources Development Fund (PSMB / Pembangunan Sumber Manusia Berhad).
rows.Keyword Patterns:
• 'PEMBANGUNAN SUMBER MANUSIA' (full Malay name)
• 'PSMB'
• 'HRDF'

JomPAY global rule applies.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
JomPAY global rule applies (see C06).
Add exclusion logic + salary ratio as validation.
rows.Additional Logic:
~1% of salary for companies with 10+ employees. May be quarterly.
Often missing for small companies — not flagged as error.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: Often missing for small companies — not flagged as error
rows.Impact on Net Cr/Dr: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)

rows.Description & Purpose:
Loan proceeds and factoring/invoice financing advances credited to account.

Two-tier approach:
Tier 1 (FULL CODE): LOAN DISB, FINANCING DISB, TRADE FINANCE CR, F ADVANCE + known_factoring_entities[].
Tier 2 (AI ASSIST): ADVANCE from unrecognised entity → flag for review.

Bank entity rule: credit from bank ≠ automatic C10. Description keyword determines. FINANCING DISB = C10, REFUND = not C10, PROFIT PAYMENT = C12.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
Bank loan: 'LOAN DISB', 'FINANCING DISB', 'TRADE FINANCE CR'
Factoring: 'F ADVANCE' or 'ADVANCE' + known_factoring_entities[]

CIMB: 'IBG CREDIT F ADVANCE ... PLANWORTH GLOBAL'
CIMB: 'AUTOPAY CR ... F ADVANCE ... PLANWORTH GLOBAL'

Planworth rule (Q4): Only classify as C10 when description contains 'F ADVANCE' or 'ADVANCE'. AUTOPAY CR from Planworth WITHOUT advance keyword = potential surplus refund, stays in net credits.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: Customer advance payments (one-off, small)
EXCLUDE: Staff advances returned (e.g. 'Advance DMC' from staff → C03 if related party)
EXCLUDE: Planworth AUTOPAY CR without 'F ADVANCE'/'ADVANCE' keyword
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): r"(?:LOAN DISB|FINANCING DISB|TRADE FINANCE CR|F ADVANCE.*(?:" + '|'.join(known_factoring_entities) + "))"
rows.Additional Logic:
Factoring = company borrows against receivables. EXCLUDED from net credits.
Identify factoring companies: recurring credits with 'ADVANCE'/'F ADVANCE' from same entity, typically RM50K-200K per txn.

Luqman still considering expanding keywords (FACTORING, FACTORING DISBURSEMENT) — parked for now.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If 'ADVANCE' keyword but entity unknown → flag for review
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Must not confuse factoring advances with customer advance payments or staff advances.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Loan/financing repayments (principal + interest/profit).

Dual-tag: C02 + C11 can co-exist. Net debit exclusion comes from C02, not C11. C11 is for REPORTING — helps analyst see total loan servicing cost.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'TR IBG [company_name] Term loan' (dual-tag C02+C11)
CIMB: 'TR IBG [company_name] Monthly Instalment' (dual-tag C02+C11)
CIMB: Recurring JomPAY biller code (same code monthly)

Generic: 'LOAN REPAY', 'FINANCING REPAY', 'INSTALMENT' (when to own company account)

JomPAY global rule: biller code must be clearly defined as loan repayment, or entity name visible.
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: 'TR IBG [related party] Instalment' — director's personal loan paid by company = C04, NOT C11.
Example: 'TR IBG SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMS House Instalment' = C04
'TR IBG MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN Monthly Instalment' = C11

JomPAY: must be very clear. If ambiguous → leave as regular expense.
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): r"(?:TERM LOAN|LOAN REPAY|FINANCING REPAY|MONTHLY INSTALMENT)" + own-party check
rows.Additional Logic:
Company's own loan: description has company_name + loan keyword → C02 + C11.
Director's personal loan: description has related_party_name + instalment → C04 only.

JomPAY biller code: recurring same code monthly = likely loan repayment, but only if clearly defined.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Fallback Rule: If JomPAY biller code unknown → leave as regular expense
rows.Cross-Ref Check: C02 exclusion already removes from net debits. C11 does NOT additionally exclude.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Fixed deposit maturities, sweep-in, interest/profit credits.

Credit side only. Same keywords (PROFIT, INTEREST, HIBAH) on debit side = C11 (loan repayment), not C12.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
'FD MATURITY', 'FIXED DEPOSIT', 'FD UPLIFT', 'INTEREST CREDIT', 'PROFIT CREDIT', 'SWEEP IN', 'HIBAH' (Islamic profit)

Note: 'CLF Pft' on debit side = financing profit (loan repayment), NOT FD interest.
rows.Exclusion Patterns: Same keywords on DEBIT side = C11 (loan repayment, not FD).
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): r"(?:FD MATUR|FIXED DEPOSIT|FD UPLIFT|INTEREST CREDIT|PROFIT CREDIT|SWEEP IN|\bHIBAH\b)"
rows.Additional Logic:
Islamic banks: 'PROFIT' or 'HIBAH' instead of 'INTEREST'.
Default to 0 if none detected (reporting logic, not classification error).

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Bank reversals — credits reversing a previous erroneous debit.

Separate from C16 (failed transfer return) — different root cause.
rows.Keyword Patterns: 'REVERSAL', 'REVERSED', 'REV CR', 'CREDIT REVERSAL'
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: 'IBG INWARD RETURN' → C16
EXCLUDE: 'GIRO INWARD RETURN' → C16
rows.Additional Logic:
IBG INWARD RETURN / GIRO INWARD RETURN are returned failed transfers — see C16.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Do not confuse with C16 (returned transfers).

rows.Description & Purpose:
Cheques deposited that bounced. Bank debits to reverse the earlier credit.

Count + amount tracked separately. NOT excluded from net debits — they naturally net off (debit out + credit back = zero).
rows.Keyword Patterns: 'RETURN CHQ', 'RETURNED CHEQUE', 'CHQ RETURN', 'DISHONOURED'
rows.Additional Logic:
Count + amount tracked. Not excluded from net debits.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose: Company's own cheques bounced. Credit reversal. Major red flag if detected.
rows.Keyword Patterns: Same keywords as C14 but CREDIT side
rows.Additional Logic:
Major red flag if detected.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Failed outward transfers returning money to account. Money never actually left — the original debit bounced and credits back.

Separate from C13 (true bank reversals). Both excluded from net credits.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'IBG INWARD RETURN'
Maybank: 'GIRO INWARD RETURN'
rows.Exclusion Patterns: Not C13 (true reversal). Distinct root cause: C16 = failed transfer to external party.
rows.Additional Logic:
Per Q1 decision (Meesha): Excluded from net credits. These inflate gross credits but the money never actually left.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.
rows.Cross-Ref Check: Separate from C13 but both excluded from net credits.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Physical cash deposits.

AML monitoring: count frequency for cash deposit transactions as part of Anti Money Laundering monitoring tools.
rows.Keyword Patterns: 'CDM CASH DEPOSIT', 'CASH DEPOSIT'
rows.Additional Logic:
AML monitoring flag. Count + amount tracked separately.
High frequency in travel industry is normal (DMC: 31 deposits in 6 months).

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Physical cash withdrawn via cash cheque.

CRITICAL distinction: CASH CHQ DR = C18 (cash withdrawal). HOUSE CHQ DR / CLRG CHQ DR = C20 (cheque issue). Prefix before 'CHQ DR' distinguishes them.
rows.Keyword Patterns: CIMB: 'CASH CHQ DR', 'CASH CHQ DR WD'
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: Regular cheques (HOUSE CHQ DR, CLRG CHQ DR → C20)
Regex to exclude any cheque that is not prefixed with 'CASH'.
rows.Additional Logic:
Prefix-based distinction:
CASH CHQ DR = C18 (cash withdrawal)
HOUSE CHQ DR = C20 (cheque issue)
CLRG CHQ DR = C20 (cheque issue)
Document explicitly for developers.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Cheques deposited by the company.

IMPORTANT: Never use 'HSE CHQ DEPOSIT' or '2D LOCAL CHQ' as counterparty name in top_parties. These are payment METHODS, not entities. Counterparty = 'Unidentified (Cheque)'.
rows.Keyword Patterns: 'HSE CHQ DEPOSIT', '2D LOCAL CHQ', 'CHQ DEPOSIT', 'CHEQUE DEPOSIT'
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): r"(?:HSE CHQ DEPOSIT|2D LOCAL CHQ|CHQ DEPOSIT|CHEQUE DEPOSIT)"
rows.Additional Logic:
Counterparty = 'Unidentified (Cheque)' — never use as top party name.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Company's cheques that cleared.

Separate from C18 (cash cheque withdrawal). Prefix distinguishes: HOUSE CHQ DR / CLRG CHQ DR = C20.
rows.Keyword Patterns: CIMB: 'HOUSE CHQ DR', 'CLRG CHQ DR'
rows.Exclusion Patterns:
EXCLUDE: 'CASH CHQ DR' → C18
EXCLUDE: 'CHQ PROCESSING FEE' → C24
rows.Additional Logic:
Separate from cash cheque withdrawal (C18).

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose: Credits that are exact multiples of RM10,000. Structuring/AML monitoring FLAG — not a classification category.
rows.Keyword Patterns: Math: amount >= 10000 AND amount % 10000 == 0
rows.Additional Logic:
Pure math — no keyword matching. Monitoring flag only.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Credits > 3× monthly average EOD balance. Relative threshold — changes monthly per company.

Fallback: If EOD average unavailable or unreliable (reconciliation FAIL), skip C22 flagging entirely. Do NOT use proxy values (opening balance, etc.). Better to miss flag than flag incorrectly.
rows.Keyword Patterns: Math: credit > 3 × eod_average_for_month
rows.Additional Logic:
Threshold varies per month.
If eod_average unavailable → skip (no proxy).
Monitoring flag only.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Credits >= RM100,000 (user-configurable threshold, default RM100,000). Absolute fixed threshold.

Distinct from C22 (relative to company). Listed individually for analyst review.
rows.Keyword Patterns: Math: credit >= threshold (default 100000)
rows.Additional Logic:
User-configurable threshold. Listed individually.
Monitoring flag only.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Service charges, processing fees, SST. Valid operating costs.

Include schema for bank fees and charges tracking.
rows.Keyword Patterns:
CIMB: 'AUTOPAY CHARGES', 'OTHER TRANSFER FEE', 'CHQ PROCESSING FEE', '3RD PARTY CHEQUE ENCASHMENT', 'ACCOUNT STATUS CONFIRMATION', 'SERVICE TAX 8% SST'
Maybank: Typically embedded in transfer fees
rows.Regex Pattern (Python): r"(?:AUTOPAY CHARGES|OTHER TRANSFER FEE|CHQ PROCESSING FEE|3RD PARTY CHEQUE|ACCOUNT STATUS CONFIRM|SERVICE TAX \d+%)"
rows.Additional Logic:
Small amounts. NOT classified as salary, statutory, or any other exclusion category.
Valid operating costs — not misclassified.

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Description & Purpose:
Statement balance markers. MUST be excluded from all processing FIRST before any classification.

DUAL PURPOSE depending on bank format:
• Banks WITH header/summary: C25 rows are duplicates → extract balances from header, remove C25 rows.
• Banks WITHOUT header/summary: C25 rows ARE the only source of opening/closing balance → extract amounts FIRST then remove from transaction list.

If no header AND no C25 rows found → opening/closing balance unavailable, reconciliation cannot run for that month.
rows.Keyword Patterns: 'CLOSING BALANCE', 'BAKI PENUTUP', 'OPENING BALANCE', 'BAKI PEMBUKAAN'
rows.Additional Logic:
Filter out FIRST before any classification. Priority P0.
Parser must handle both formats (with and without header/summary).

Dropdown Entity Selection Rule: deduplicate company options using normalized name (trim spaces, collapse whitespace, uppercase, strip punctuation/company suffix variants) and always store selection with explicit party_type label OWN_PARTY or RELATED_PARTY.

rows.Category: THREE EXECUTION MODES: All 25 classification rules serve THREE modes from ONE rulebook: (1) Full AI — Claude classifies from raw descriptions. (2) Optimised AI — Python handles deterministic rules first, AI handles fuzzy judgment only. (3) Full Code — everything in Python, zero AI. Execution Mode column indicates readiness: FULL CODE = works in all 3 modes. AI ASSIST = needs AI in modes 1 & 2, exhaustive rules needed for mode 3.

JOMPAY GLOBAL RULE: JomPAY is a payment channel, NOT a payee. Never classify based on JomPAY biller code alone. Only classify when entity name is visible in description. If description is just 'JOMPAY xxxx:xxxxx' with no entity name → leave as regular expense. Applies to C06, C07, C08, C09, and C11.

CLASSIFICATION RULE ORDER: C25 (filter balance rows) → C01/C02 (own party) → C05 (salary) → C03/C04 (related party) → C06-C09 (statutory) → C10 (loan disb) → C11 (loan repay) → C12-C13 (FD/reversal) → C14-C16 (returned cheques/returns) → C17-C20 (cash/cheque) → C24 (bank fees) → remainder = income/expense
NO 'UNKNOWN' BUCKET: If no exclusion matches, transaction is income (credit side) or expense (debit side). It stays in net credits/debits.
DUAL-TAGGING: A single transaction can carry two category tags (e.g. C02 + C11). Excluded from net debits only ONCE via C02. C11 tracked separately for reporting.
COMPANY-AGNOSTIC: Rules use variables (company_name, related_parties[], known_factoring_entities[]) — not hardcoded names. Test data: MUHAFIZ (CIMB) and DMC (Maybank Islamic).

UNCLASSIFIED TRANSACTIONS: Description too vague/missing to determine type. Track monthly: unclassified_cr_count, unclassified_cr_amount, unclassified_dr_count, unclassified_dr_amount. List individually when single transaction > RM10,000 (user-configurable). Stays in net credits/debits — NOT excluded.

FX DEFAULT RULE: Default to NOT FX unless clear conversion evidence exists. TT CREDIT = transfer method, NOT currency indicator. RENTAS/JANM = domestic MYR. Alphabet codes (GBPV, USDP) in reference fields = internal voucher codes, NOT currencies.

v3.1 STATUTORY COVERAGE VALIDATION: Statutory validation uses TWO layers. Layer 1 (PRIMARY) — Coverage Check: For every month where salary_paid > 0, check whether each statutory field (EPF, SOCSO, LHDN) is also > 0. Missing = GAP. Layer 2 (SECONDARY) — Per-Month Ratio Check: Only where BOTH salary and statutory exist in same month. EPF 8-16% OK, SOCSO 1-5% OK. Ratios above range may indicate lump-sum catch-up. NEVER compute ratio as total_statutory / total_salary across all months — this masks gaps. Output in consolidated.statutory_compliance with missing month lists and per-month ratios. See V7-V10 in Tab 6.

v3.1 SOCSO FALLBACK FIX: Previous threshold 'salary > RM50K' for SOCSO warning was too lenient. SOCSO is mandatory regardless of salary quantum. Removed the RM50K threshold. Now: if salary_paid > 0 in ANY month but statutory_socso = 0 → flag WARNING with missing month list.

rows.Description: If user provides related_parties[] list, use as PRIMARY source. Direct list lookup — highest authority.
rows.Logic: Direct list lookup against related_parties[] array.
rows.Edge Case: None — user is authoritative. Need to be careful with regex — make it flexible for name variations.

rows.Description:
Extract distinctive/unique word(s) from company_name. Other entities sharing root → Sister Company CANDIDATE.

Root = the distinctive/unique word in the company name, not fixed 'first 2 words'. Short but unique (DMC, 3 chars) = sufficient. Short but generic (GLOBAL, PRIME, MEGA) = needs more words.

Based on Malaysian company naming conventions.
rows.Logic:
root = distinctive/unique words (skip SDN/BHD/&/CO/PTY/LTD)
MUHAFIZ → matches MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY, MUHAFIZ PRIMA
DMC TRAVEL → 'DMC' is unique enough
rows.Example: MUHAFIZ → matches MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY, MUHAFIZ PRIMA
rows.Relationship: Sister Company (CANDIDATE — validate by transaction pattern)
rows.Edge Case: Short but unique (DMC) = sufficient root. Short but generic (GLOBAL) = needs more words. Root name match = candidate only, validate by transaction frequency/pattern.

rows.Description:
Person names appearing in recurring debits with personal keywords → Director.

Purpose keyword is tiebreaker: same person receiving salary (C05) vs personal expenses (C04).
Two-way financial behaviour (both credits AND debits) = potential related party for analyst review.
rows.Logic: Match account holder / signatory names in debit descriptions. Check purpose keyword to disambiguate.
rows.Example:
SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMSI → 'TR TO C/A SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAM'
MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN → salary + operational expenses
rows.Edge Case: In travel companies, directors may ALSO be operational staff. MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN: Salary = C05, operational = regular expense, Advance/Advance DMC = C03. Per Q8.

rows.Description:
Individual names in 3+ debits with personal keywords: PETTY CASH, CREDIT CARD, HOUSING LOAN, etc.

Frequency alone ≠ related party. Frequency + personal keywords = strong signal.
rows.Logic: Count(name in debit desc) >= 3 AND personal_keyword present
rows.Example: DAYANG SITI RAUDZAH (33 txns) with HOUSING LOAN, MDM661, PETTY CASH
rows.Edge Case: MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN (57 txns at DMC) — split classification per Q8. HALA GATEWAYS (17 txns, RM516K) = supplier, NOT related party.

rows.Description:
Same person with different name formats must resolve to one entity.

3-tier deterministic matching:
Tier 1: Exact match on normalised name
Tier 2: Starts-with match (min 10 chars)
Tier 3: Token containment (skip BIN/BINTI/BT)
rows.Logic: Match on first name + 3+ chars of patronymic
rows.Example: SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMSI / SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAM / SHAHARUDDIN SAMSI → same person
rows.Edge Case: BIN/BINTI variations, truncation after 20 chars in Maybank (* truncation).

rows.Description:
Never auto-detect these as related parties.

Exclude: govt (JANM/LHDN/KWSP/PERKESO/PSMB), banks, utilities, known suppliers, factoring companies.

Frequency alone ≠ related party.
rows.Logic: Exclude: govt, banks, utilities, known suppliers, factoring cos. Search for factoring companies in descriptions.
rows.Example:
PLANWORTH GLOBAL = NOT related (factoring co)
HALA GATEWAYS (17 txns, RM516K) = supplier, NOT related
rows.Edge Case: HALA GATEWAYS = supplier despite high frequency.

rows.Logic: Q7 — Luqman confirmed. CR RM98,342 (C03) + DR RM144,803 (C04)

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'IBG CREDIT', skip ref codes (<8 chars alphanumeric), take remaining words as entity name.
rows.Example Input: IBG CREDIT PW0825353 PW0825353 PERBENA EMAS SDN. BH
rows.Edge Case: Ref codes can look like names. Skip tokens that are all-caps alphanumeric <8 chars.
rows.Status: LOCKED — Luqman: try full code first

rows.How to Extract: Factoring advance. Extract company name after 'F ADVANCE'. Normalise FAC → FACTORING.
rows.Example Input: IBG CREDIT F ADVANCE F ADVANCE PLANWORTH GLOBAL FAC
rows.Edge Case: All F ADVANCE from same entity = same factoring company. Be aware of other patterns in future.

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT', skip purpose words (ONLINE TRANSFER, CASH, PAYMENT), extract entity name.
rows.Example Input: DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT ONLINE TRANSFER NS BLUESCOPE LYSAGHT
rows.Edge Case: Purpose-word filtering must be careful. Note debit or credit side.

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'AUTOPAY CR', skip batch ref (duplicated), take entity name.
rows.Example Input: AUTOPAY CR BM250822 BM250822 PIASAU GAS SDN BHD
rows.Edge Case: Batch refs are duplicated — skip both copies.
All JANM = government. NOT FX. Flexible location (not hardcoded to KUCHING).

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'TRANSFER TO A/C', take name before *, purpose after *.
rows.Example Input: TRANSFER TO A/C NUR ATIKAH BINTI ZA* Umrah atikah 1 DMC
rows.Edge Case: Name truncated at * — fuzzy matching Tier 2 needed. Depends on execution mode.

rows.How to Extract: Skip prefix, extract entity/person name. Use stop rules (numbers, MBB, CT) not fixed word count.
rows.Example Input: TR TO C/A MUHAFIZ TECHNOLOGY SHAHARUDDIN B SAMSI
rows.Edge Case: May have purpose text after name. Depends on execution mode.

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'TRANSFER FR A/C', take name before *, purpose after *.
rows.Example Input: TRANSFER FR A/C HALA GATEWAYS ENTER* INV00608 MBB CT
rows.Edge Case: Same truncation as CP7. Depends on execution mode.

rows.How to Extract: Skip 'PAYMENT FR A/C', skip card/account number, extract entity after *. Check statutory (C06/C07) first.
rows.Edge Case: EPF/SOCSO also use this pattern — check C06/C07 before counterparty extraction.

rows.How to Extract: CANNOT extract counterparty. Tag as 'Unidentified (Cheque)'. NEVER use as top party name.
rows.Example Input: CDM CASH DEPOSIT / HSE CHQ DEPOSIT / 2D LOCAL CHQ
rows.Edge Case: NEVER use cheque labels as counterparty. Count frequency separately.

rows.How to Extract:
After extraction: trim refs, standardise SDN BHD, deduplicate.

Changed to AI ASSIST per Luqman: simple cleanup (punctuation, uppercase, SDN/BHD) can be coded. Merging truncated names needs AI judgment. Only normalise when confident of match. Wrong normalisation is worse than duplicates.
rows.Example Input: PLANWORTH GLOBAL FAC → PLANWORTH GLOBAL FACTORING SDN BHD
rows.Edge Case: Normalisation should not be mandatory — could be wrong. Only normalise when confident.
rows.Status: LOCKED — changed to AI ASSIST per Luqman

rows.Operation: NET CREDITS = gross_credits - C01 - C03 - C13 - C10 - C12 - C16
Net Credits formula check: gross - exclusions = net (exact match)
Net Debits formula check: gross - exclusions = net (exact match)
EPF ÷ Salary should be 11-13% PER MONTH (where BOTH exist)
rows.Formula: WARNING if outside 8-16%. CATCH_UP if > 16%. NEVER use aggregate ratio.

rows.Operation: SOCSO ÷ Salary should be 2-3% PER MONTH (where BOTH exist)
rows.Formula: WARNING if outside 1-5%. CATCH_UP if > 5%. NEVER use aggregate ratio.

rows.Operation: No double-counting: C02+C11 only excludes ONCE from net debits
Sum of monthly net_credits = consolidated net_credits
EPF coverage: every month with salary_paid > 0 should have statutory_epf > 0
rows.Formula:
Count months_with_salary vs months_with_epf.
Missing EPF in any payroll month = compliance gap.
Report: epf_months_paid / salary_months_active and list missing months.

rows.Operation: SOCSO coverage: every month with salary_paid > 0 should have statutory_socso > 0
rows.Formula:
Count months_with_salary vs months_with_socso.
Missing SOCSO in any payroll month = compliance gap.
Report: socso_months_paid / salary_months_active and list missing months.

rows.Operation: LHDN coverage: if salary exists across period, at least one LHDN payment expected
rows.Formula:
LHDN may legitimately be paid from another account.
Flag as informational if zero, not as hard compliance failure.

rows.Operation: Statutory payment gap summary: aggregate V7-V9 into statutory_compliance object in consolidated
rows.Formula:
Downstream HTML renderer uses this to show statutory compliance dashboard.
Overall status: COMPLIANT / GAPS_DETECTED / CRITICAL.

rows.Question: IBG/GIRO Inward Return — exclude from net credits?
rows.Decision: YES — exclude. C16 is separate from C13 but both excluded from net credits. Added as Step 7 in net credits formula.
rows.Impact:
MUHAFIZ: RM18,622 (9 txns)
DMC: RM780 (1 txn)

rows.Question: CREDIT ADV (RM4,504, no description) — how to classify?
rows.Decision: Option A with tracking. Unclassified stays in net credits/debits. Track monthly (unclassified_cr_count/amount, unclassified_dr_count/amount). List individually when > RM10K (user-configurable).

rows.Question: TR TO SAVINGS (91 txns, RM316K) — all salary?
rows.Decision: Option C — NOT auto-salary. AUTOPAY DR captures bulk salary. Classify individually: extract name → check related party (C04) → check salary keywords → if match = C05 → remainder = regular expense.

rows.Question: Planworth AUTOPAY CR without 'F ADVANCE' — exclude all?
rows.Decision: Option B — only with 'F ADVANCE'/'ADVANCE'. AUTOPAY CR without advance keyword = potential surplus refund, stays in net credits. Applies to all factoring companies.

rows.Question: SHAUFIAH NUR ASHIKIN (6 txns RM19K) — relationship?
rows.Decision: Family Member — mother of SHAHARUDDIN (director). All 6 txns = C04.

rows.Question: DAYANG SITI RAUDZAH (33 txns RM179K) — relationship?
rows.Decision: Family Member — wife of SHAHARUDDIN (director). All 33 txns = C04. Total RM179,632 (not RM44K as originally listed — TR TO SAVINGS added 21 more).

rows.Question: NUR THAHIRAH NAZIHA (90 txns, RM244K) — who?
MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN (57 txns, RM190K) — classification?
rows.Decision: Option C — split. Salary (RM3,089.15/month) = C05. Operational (visa, tickets) = regular expense. Credits 'Advance'/'Advance DMC' = C03. Credits 'Refund'/'Return' = excluded. RP3 behavioural detection.

rows.Question: TT CREDIT WITHOUT FLOAT (RM71,590) — is this FX?
rows.Decision: Do NOT assume FX. TT = transfer method, not currency indicator. Flag as potential FX for analyst review. Default to NOT FX unless clear evidence.

rows.Question: STAFF INCENTIVE (RM10K) — salary or bonus?
rows.Decision: Option A — include under C05. Staff incentive = employee compensation. STAFF INCENTIVE, STAFF OVERTIME, STAFF BONUS, STAFF ADVANCE, EXTRA SALARY = all C05.

rows.Notes: MUHAFIZ: AUTOPAY DR bulk. DMC: individual transfers
DMC: not detected (may be paid elsewhere)
DMC: flagged as potential FX per Q9, not confirmed
